# Canonical-tool FAT32 fixture
#
# Source image: image.fat32.gz (gzip of a 32 MiB FAT32 volume).
# Built on macOS with mtools `mformat` (FAT32 minimum cluster count
# of ~65525 makes 32 MiB the smallest legal volume — newfs_msdos and
# mkfs.vfat agree). The compressed fixture is ~33 KiB on disk and
# expands to 33 554 432 bytes.
#
# Reproduce locally (mtools >= 4):
#
#   dd if=/dev/zero of=image.img bs=1024 count=1024
#   cat > .mtoolsrc <<'EOF'
#   drive z: file="image.img"
#   mtools_skip_check=1
#   EOF
#   MTOOLSRC=.mtoolsrc mformat -F -v TESTFAT32 -C \
#                              -t 128 -h 16 -n 32 -c 1 \
#                              -N DEADBEEF z:
#   printf "Hello from mkfs FAT32 test!\n"                                 > hello.txt
#   printf "Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit.\n"    > notes.txt
#   printf "Second line for FAT32 testing.\n"                             >> notes.txt
#   MTOOLSRC=.mtoolsrc mcopy hello.txt z:/HELLO.TXT
#   MTOOLSRC=.mtoolsrc mcopy notes.txt z:/NOTES.TXT
#   gzip -9 image.img
#   mv image.img.gz testdata/mkfs/image.fat32.gz
#
# Properties that TestReadMkfsImage asserts:
#
#   volume-label  = "TESTFAT32"
#   volume-id     = 0xDEADBEEF
#   total-bytes   = 33554432
#   sectors       = 65536  (512 bytes/sector)
#   files
#     /HELLO.TXT  28 bytes, sha256
#                 0fb70af07b4a82c32af7bf602d3e9bb533ba3b3b9c55aec030a3880e93d8f319
#     /NOTES.TXT  88 bytes, sha256
#                 9a616954bcb237e505134e9cfa512e90983b22c5b2dc651191b35042b6dffb1c
